question: Are flash loans possible on Stellar or Soroban, and how would atomic multi-contract logic work in one transaction?

golden.answer:
Use `stellarDocs.search_asset_token_docs`, `stellarDocs.search_protocol_concepts_docs`, `stellarDocs.search_sdk_cli_tools_docs`, `stellarDocs.search_soroban_contract_docs` to perform the current documented lookup or workflow for this question. Report the source and as-of date for the observed documentation or response. Treat version, UI, provider, and availability statements as observations of that retrieval, not timeless facts.

Stable guardrails that remain in force: Explains atomic Soroban cross-contract flash-loan mechanics and rollback constraints. Distinguishes verified deployed Blend v2 from XycLoans code/history and Slender marketing/unverified usability.
keyFacts: Executes the current documented lookup/workflow via stellarDocs.search_asset_token_docs, stellarDocs.search_protocol_concepts_docs, stellarDocs.search_sdk_cli_tools_docs, stellarDocs.search_soroban_contract_docs and labels version, UI, provider, and availability claims as observed.
Explains atomic Soroban cross-contract flash-loan mechanics and rollback constraints.
Distinguishes verified deployed Blend v2 from XycLoans code/history and Slender marketing/unverified usability.
avoid: Do NOT claim XycLoans is the sole/current mainnet implementation or that Slender has no flash-loan association.
Do NOT treat source code, marketing, issue status, or directory status alone as deployment/liquidity proof.
Do NOT state a specific current version, UI, provider, or availability observation as a timeless fact.
golden.notes:
CORRECTED 2026-06-29: prior version misattributed flash loans to Slender. Live Scout confirms Slender is a noncustodial lending/borrowing money market (by eq-lab), NOT a flash-loan protocol. XycLoans is the credible Soroban flash-loans protocol ('a decentralized flash loans protocol that runs on Soroban... a flash loans-only protocol'), audited by OtterSec Jan 2024 (sorobansecurity.com/report/33). Slender reference removed; XycLoans is the named flash-loan provider, with dated/source evidence required before treating as production-safe.
Also good if the answer: Names XycLoans as the credible Soroban flash-loans protocol (flash-loans-only, per-token liquidity pools enforcing same-transaction repayment), with current evidence required for any live-availability claim. | Mentions simulation/testing for resource limits and failure behavior.
Golden cites: Stellar/Soroban transaction and contract invocation docs, plus dated protocol docs for any live flash-loan implementation.
Corrected 2026-07-10. Deployment evidence tiers—not an exclusive project name—are the factual gate.

corroboration.claim: Soroban flash loans are atomic call-tree patterns and Blend v2 exposes a verified mainnet flash_loan interface
